assessment_items.question_content:
**Rubens' work for Marie de Medici was grandiose and over-the-top. Rubens combined representations of Marie de Medici's life events with . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ef6041b8821ab2634eee15e69cbe3734.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The commission was grandiose and Rubens combined representations of Marie de Medici's life events with many mythological subjects.

[Watch the video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/flanders-1/v/rubens-arrival-or-disembarkation-of-marie-de-medici-at-marseilles-1621-25)

assessment_items.question_content:
**In Rubens' *The Rape of the Daughters of Leucippus*, there are no visible brushstrokes and the coloration of the flesh is muted.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/725fd47b3bf6edd4d1fdebae23976eb6.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Rubens is well known for the complex coloration of human flesh and loose visible brush work. The skin of the daughters of Leucippus for example are rendered in greens and yellows and blues with orange and pink.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/flanders-1/v/peter-paul-rubens-the-rape-of-the-daughters-of-leucippus)

The painting emphasizes the suffering, violence, and horrors of war.

[Watch this video for review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/flanders-1/v/peter-paul-rubens-the-consequences-of-war-1638-39)

choices.content: the suffering, violence, and horrors of war.
the heroism and energy of war.
the bravery and cowardice of war.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Rubens' paintings for Marie de Medici represented . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ef6041b8821ab2634eee15e69cbe3734.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Rubens and his workshop painted 24 paintings for Marie de Medici. The paintings commemorate the major events of Marie de Medici's life.

[Watch the video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/flanders-1/v/rubens-arrival-or-disembarkation-of-marie-de-medici-at-marseilles-1621-25)

choices.content: the major events of Marie de Medici's life.
the major historical events that involved the French monarchy.
scenes from the life of St. Martin of Tours, the patron saint of France.
scenes from the lives of Sts. Cosmas and Damian, the patron saints of the Medici family.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Rubens' *The Consequences of War* was commissioned by a Flemish member of the Medici court during which war?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ef507d4b8ba9b007afaff67f360d46c5.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This painting was commissioned by a Flemish member of the Medici court during the Thirty Years' War.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/flanders-1/v/peter-paul-rubens-the-consequences-of-war-1638-39)

choices.content: The War with Spain for Flemish independance

hints:
Baroque art is interested in movement. In Baroque art, forms push outward and break the picture plane and compositions often used diagonal lines.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/flanders-1/v/peter-paul-rubens-the-rape-of-the-daughters-of-leucippus)

choices.content: created forms that pushed outward and broke through the picture plane
created compositions that emphasized horizontal and vertical lines
created works of art with a calm and peaceful tone

assessment_items.question_content:
**In Rubens' *The Rape of the Daughters of Leucippus*, the composition forms . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/725fd47b3bf6edd4d1fdebae23976eb6.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The composition is constructed with two intersecting diagonal lines.

[Watch this video to revivew](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/flanders-1/v/peter-paul-rubens-the-rape-of-the-daughters-of-leucippus)

**Rubens'* The Consequences of War* is both . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ef507d4b8ba9b007afaff67f360d46c5.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The battle scene is both mythological and allegorical.
